title: The DAO — reentrancy drains $60M ETH; led to the ETH/ETC fork
note: Recursive call to splitDAO before balance update. Fix by hard-fork; chain split into ETH (rollback) and ETC (immutable).

title: Parity Multisig — initWallet exploit drains 153k ETH (~$30M)
note: Vulnerable initWallet function let any address claim ownership of a deployed multisig. Three high-profile multisigs drained.

title: Parity library suicide bricks ~$300M of ETH permanently
note: User devops199 self-destructed the shared library contract that 587 multisig wallets delegated to. Funds frozen, never recovered.

title: bZx — first major flashloan attack on DeFi (~$355k)
note: First widely covered flashloan exploit. Manipulated WBTC/ETH oracle on Uniswap via flashloan. Followed by a second similar attack four days later.

title: dForce / Lendf.Me — imBTC reentrancy drains $25M (later returned)
note: ERC-777 callback used to reenter Lendf.Me's token deposits. Attacker doxxed; returned all funds.

title: Harvest Finance — Curve oracle manipulation drains $24M
note: Manipulated USDT/USDC pool prices via flashloan to inflate share value, then redeemed for profit.

title: Pickle Finance — evil jar swap drains $19.7M from cDAI strategy
note: Attacker registered a malicious jar that the controller treated as legitimate, then swapped real DAI out for a worthless token.

title: Yearn v1 yDAI — $11M lost via Curve 3pool imbalance flashloan
note: Attacker repeatedly imbalanced + rebalanced Curve 3pool to skim from yDAI vault rebases. ~$2.8M net profit on $11M loss.

title: Poly Network — cross-chain bridge drained for $611M; attacker returned all funds
note: Manipulated EthCrossChainManager to override the keeper public key, then minted unlimited bridge withdrawals. Attacker eventually returned the entire amount.

title: Compound — proposal 062 misallocates $90M+ COMP via comptroller bug
note: Not an attack — a governance upgrade introduced a logic bug that paid out far more COMP than intended. Some recipients refused to return funds.

title: Cream Finance — yUSDVault price manipulation drains $130M
note: Third major Cream exploit in 2021. Manipulated yUSDVault.pricePerShare via direct token transfers to bloat collateral value, then borrowed against fake collateral.

title: BadgerDAO — front-end exploit drains $130M via injected approve tx
note: Attacker injected malicious script into Badger's Cloudflare-served front-end, prompting users to sign unlimited token approvals on the attacker's address.

title: Wormhole — signature verification bypass mints 120k wETH on Solana
note: Attacker spoofed a guardian signature set on the Solana side, minted 120k wETH unbacked, then bridged to Ethereum. Jump Crypto refilled the bridge.

title: Ronin Bridge — $625M Lazarus Group validator-key compromise
note: 5 of 9 Ronin validators compromised via spear-phishing. Attacker signed off on fraudulent withdrawals from the Ethereum-side bridge contract. Attributed to DPRK Lazarus Group.

title: Beanstalk — governance flashloan drains $182M via emergencyCommit
note: Attacker took a flashloan to acquire majority governance, then passed and instantly executed a malicious proposal that drained the protocol's reserves.

title: Inverse Finance — Keep3r oracle manipulation drains $15.6M
note: Manipulated INV/ETH price on Sushiswap (which Keep3r used as an oracle) via large swap, then borrowed and stole DOLA against the inflated INV collateral.

title: Harmony Horizon Bridge — $100M drained via 2-of-5 multisig key compromise
note: Attacker compromised 2 of 5 multisig signers controlling the Ethereum-side bridge. Lazarus Group attribution by US Treasury (Jan 2023).

title: Nomad Bridge — $190M drained in copy-paste free-for-all
note: Init bug treated unsigned messages as valid. Once the first attacker showed the way, hundreds of opportunists ran the same tx with their own address — the attack became a public free-for-all.

title: Wintermute — $160M drained from market-maker hot wallet via Profanity vanity-address grind
note: Wintermute used a vanity address generated by the Profanity tool, which had been disclosed as having a guessable seed. Attacker brute-forced the private key.

title: Mango Markets — Avraham Eisenberg oracle manipulation, $114M (Solana, NOT EVM — informational)
note: INFORMATIONAL ONLY — Solana, will be filtered out at seed time. Listed for completeness because it's referenced widely.

title: BNB Chain — Token Hub IAVL proof forgery mints $586M of BNB
note: Forged a Merkle proof against a vulnerable IAVL implementation, minting 2M BNB cross-chain. Validators paused the chain mid-attack to limit damage.

title: Euler — donateToReserves donation accounting bug drains $197M
note: donateToReserves let the attacker forfeit collateral while keeping liability, triggering an under-collateralized liquidation against themselves at a profit. Attacker returned 100% of funds after months of negotiation.

title: Multichain — MPC keys compromised, $231M drained from bridges
note: MPC node keys controlled by Multichain's CEO were compromised after his arrest in China. Funds drained from multiple bridge contracts across chains.

title: Curve — Vyper compiler reentrancy bug drains $73M from stable pools
note: Vyper versions 0.2.15-0.3.0 had a faulty reentrancy lock implementation. Multiple Curve pools (alETH, msETH, pETH, CRV/ETH) drained. White-hat MEV bots front-ran several attacks; some attackers returned funds.

title: Mixin — $200M drained via cloud database breach exposing hot wallet keys
note: Cloud-service-provider DB breach exposed Mixin's deposit hot-wallet keys. Funds drained across multiple chains including Ethereum.

title: KyberSwap Elastic — concentrated-liquidity tick exploit drains $48M
note: Attacker exploited an off-by-one in tick boundary handling to repeatedly cross the same liquidity range, double-counting liquidity. Multi-chain hit (Ethereum + Polygon + Arbitrum + Optimism + Base).

title: Munchables — rogue dev drains $62M; funds returned 24h later
note: An insider on the Munchables team (suspected DPRK affiliation) inserted a backdoor into the contract during development. After negotiation, funds were returned and no users lost money.

title: PlayDapp — minter role compromise mints 1.79B PLA tokens (~$290M)
note: Attacker added themselves as a minter on the PLA token contract, then minted billions of new tokens and dumped on exchanges. Exchanges froze part of the proceeds.

title: Gala Games — admin key compromise mints 5B GALA tokens (~$200M)
note: Compromised admin wallet minted 5B GALA. Project recovered ~$23M in dumped tokens by negotiation. Most of the supply was burnable.

title: LI.FI — facet upgrade exploit drains $11M from approval users
note: Attacker exploited an unverified facet in the LiFi diamond contract to call arbitrary functions on users who'd granted unlimited approvals.

title: Radiant Capital — multisig compromise drains $50M cross-chain
note: DPRK-attributed attack: 3 of 11 multisig signers had their hardware wallets compromised, signed a malicious upgrade that drained user funds.
